news.summary: Adding to its existing 66 statewide stores, Curaleaf Holdings, Inc. has opened a new location in Southwest Florida, bringing the total store count in the state to 67 and nationwide to 153. This leading international provider of consumer cannabis products is making strides in expanding its presence. This news was shared from its headquarters in Stamford, Conn., on May 23, 2025. Curaleaf Holdings, Inc., traded on the TSX under the ticker CURA and on the OTCQX as CURLF, continues to solidify its position in the market with this latest development.
news.headline: Curaleaf opens new dispensary in Punta Gorda, Florida, bringing state count to 67 and nationwide count to 153.

news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets are showing little movement on Friday morning, with the fear and greed index reaching the 'extreme greed' zone. Current prices include Bitcoin at $111,032.03 (+0.22%), Ethereum at $2,662.21 (+0.65%), Solana at $185.24 (+4.2%), XRP at $2.43 (+0.96%), Dogecoin at $0.2451 (+2.4%), and Shiba Inu at $0.00001552 (+1.9%). Notable data reveals a 26.2% increase in large transaction volume for Bitcoin and a 4.6% increase for Ethereum. BTC's daily active addresses have also risen by 5.8%. Over the past 24 hours, 103,711 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses of $247.61 million. Additionally, net inflows of $934.7 million were seen in spot Bitcoin ETFs, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw $110.5 million in net inflows. Analyst Michael van de Poppe identifies the $105,000–$108,000 zone as crucial. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Crypto markets trading sideways, fear & greed index at 'extreme greed'. Bitcoin at $111,032.03, Ethereum at $2,662.21, XRP at $2.43, Dogecoin at $0.2451, Solana at $185.24. Notable stats: BTC & ETH large transaction volumes up, 103,711 traders liquidated worth $247.61m in the past 24h. $934.7m inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs, $110.5m inflows into spot Ethereum ETFs. Michael van de Poppe sees $105,000–$108,000 zone as prime, caution urged.

Overall, this action signifies the company's commitment to adhering to market guidelines and improving its performance.

news.summary: In a recent announcement, it was reported that the total number of confirmed COVID-19 cases in the country has surpassed 500,000, with an additional 2,000 cases being reported in the last 24 hours alone. The death toll has also reached 10,000, with 50 new deaths recorded in the same period. Health officials have expressed concerns about the rapid spread of the virus and are urging the public to adhere to safety measures such as wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and frequent hand washing to mitigate the transmission. Additionally, hospitals are said to be operating at near capacity, prompting authorities to consider implementing stricter lockdown measures to curb the rising number of cases.
news.headline: Rising temperatures bring nights hotter and more mosquitoes, creating a worrisome global scenario.

news.summary: On May 23, 2025, the yearly general meeting took place in Truecaller AB in Stockholm. A rundown of the decisions made during the meeting is as follows. A resolution was passed regarding the approval of accounts and the distribution of the company's result. Shareholders agreed to adopt the financial statements and discharge the board and CEO from liability for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The decision was made to allocate a dividend of $0.50 per share, totaling $5 million. Furthermore, it was resolved to re-elect all current board members and auditors, as well as to approve the proposed remuneration policy for company executives.
news.headline: Annual general meeting held in Truecaller AB on May 23, 2025. Company's accounts & results adopted.

news.summary: GN Store Nord A/S has disclosed on May 22, 2025, in compliance with Danish Capital Markets Act Sections 38(1) and 39(1), that BlackRock, Inc. notified them of a holding increase. BlackRock, Inc. raised its combined direct and indirect ownership of shares and financial instruments on May 21, 2025. This increase pushed their total ownership in GN Store Nord A/S beyond 5% of both the share capital and voting rights, as outlined in Danish Capital Markets Act Sections 38 and 39(2)(1) and (2).
news.headline: BlackRock, Inc. increased shareholding to over 5% in GN Store Nord A/S on May 21, 2025, as per Danish Capital Markets Act.

news.summary: Bitcoin reached a new all-time high of $111,970 on May 22 before retracing to $110,700. Analysts are cautious about market overheating but suggest a healthy upward trend. The funding rate remains low compared to previous peaks, with long bets increasing but still smaller than before. Profit-taking by short-term investors is limited, indicating market overheating is negligible. The MVRV Z-score is 2.8, below the red zone, suggesting the market top is not yet in. Bitcoin's RSI is overbought in shorter timeframes but near oversold in longer ones. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index is at 78, indicating extreme greed. Caution is advised, but these metrics do not guarantee a trend reversal. Bitcoin's rally could continue with increasing spot ETF demand and easing trade war tensions. Investment decisions should be made after conducting personal research.
news.headline: Bitcoin reaches $111,970 ATH on May 22, but retraces to $110,700. Analysts analyze signals on market overheating, funding rates suggest a "healthy upward phase." Whales' profit-taking is subdued despite high price. BTC market remains optimistic; MVRV Z-score at 2.8, RSI indicates "greedy" conditions. Volatility persists amid ETF demand and trade war calm.
Hot evenings and pesky mosquitoes in a warming world.

news.summary: In Toronto on May 23, 2025, a company known as Gulf & Pacific Equities Corp. (TSX-V:  GUF) disclosed their recent financial performance. Specializing in acquiring, managing, and developing anchored shopping centers in Western Canada, they noted a noteworthy 4.1% rise in revenue. Specifically, their revenue climbed to $1,132,621 during the quarter concluding on March 31, 2025, showcasing an increase from $1,087,886 recorded during the corresponding period in the previous year.
news.headline: Gulf & Pacific Equities Corp. reports Q1 revenue increase to $1.13 million, up by 4.1% from last year. Refinancing of mortgages also highlighted.

news.summary: In Philadelphia and Vancouver, on May 23, 2025, BriaCell Therapeutics Corp. (Nasdaq: BCTX, BCTXW, BCTXZ) and (TSX: BCT), a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing innovative immunotherapies for cancer treatment, revealed encouraging survival and clinical benefit information through three clinical poster displays and a publish-only abstract at the imminent 2025 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting in Chicago, IL. The event is scheduled from May 30 to June 3 at McCormick Place.
news.headline: BriaCell presents positive data at ASCO 2025, including 3 posters and 1 abstract, at meeting from May 30 - June 3 in Chicago.

news.summary: Dublin - A recent report titled "Middle East & Africa Data Center Market Landscape 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market for data centers in the Middle East & Africa region recorded a value of USD 8.63 billion in 2024. It is anticipated to grow significantly, reaching USD 19.89 billion by 2030, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 14.93%. Noteworthy operators in this market consist of both global and local data center companies, such as Khanzna Data Centers and Gulf Dat.
news.headline: MEA Data Center Market to reach USD 19.89 Billion by 2030, growing at 14.93% CAGR from USD 8.63 Billion in 2024.

news.summary: On May 23 rd , 2025, Reykjavik Energy (Orkuveita Reykjavíkur) successfully completed a green bond sale, expanding the green bond series OR1802142 GB. The company issued ISK 5,300 million in bonds at a yield of 7.78%. With this latest offering, the total nominal value of bonds in the series has reached ISK 11,538 million.
news.headline: Reykjavik Energy concludes green bond offering, increases series to ISK 11,538 million with 7.78% yield.

news.summary: Sol-Gel Technologies, a dermatology company, reported its first-quarter financial results for 2025. Total revenue was $1 million, up from $0.5 million in 2024. Research and development expenses increased to $8.8 million, from $5.3 million in 2024 due to manufacturing development costs for SGT-610. General and administrative expenses decreased to $1.3 million from $1.8 million in 2024. The company reported a net loss of $8.8 million in Q1 2025, compared to $6.3 million in 2024. As of March 31, 2025, Sol-Gel had $16.9 million in cash. Sol-Gel expects its cash to fund requirements up to Q1 2027. Key products include TWYNEO and EPSOLAY. Sol-Gel focuses on developing products for skin diseases. The company's cash and assets are detailed in the condensed consolidated balance sheet provided. It includes a disclaimer regarding forward-looking statements and potential risks influencing the company's performance.
news.headline: Sol-Gel Technologies reported Q1 2025 results: $1 million revenue, $8.8 million R&D expenses, $1.3 million general expenses, $8.8 million net loss. Cash balance $16.9 million, projected to fund into Q1 2027. Company focuses on dermatology and has products for the treatment of acne and rosacea. Risk factors include ongoing clinical trials and regulatory approvals.

news.summary: Shanghai-based company UniXell Biotechnology Co., Ltd. recently made headlines on May 23, 2025, with the news of the successful introduction of their pioneering Parkinson's disease cell drug, UX-DA001, in China. This milestone was reached in March 2025, signifying a significant step forward in the field of cell therapies. UniXell Biotech specializes in innovative medical solutions and this recent development marks a crucial advancement for individuals suffering from Parkinson's disease.
news.headline: UniXell Biotechnology administers first dose of Parkinson's disease cell therapy in China for Phase 1 study.

news.summary: IonQ, a prominent entity in the field of commercial quantum computing and networking, has recently disclosed the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Korea Institute of Science and Technology Information (KISTI). This move signifies a noteworthy stride in IonQ's existing collaboration with South Korea, spanning across government, academic, and industry spheres. KISTI, renowned as a national science and technology research institute and supercomputing center, is a key player in this arrangement. This strategic partnership is poised to fortify IonQ's foothold in South Korea and foster further advancements in the quantum computing domain.
news.headline: IonQ partners with KISTI to boost S. Korea’s role in quantum race, strengthening ties with government, academia, and industry.

news.summary: The global panic attack treatment market is projected to grow from USD 2.92 billion in 2025 to USD 8.66 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 16.8%. The market encompasses pharmacological and non-pharmacological approaches to manage symptoms and reduce panic attack frequency. By 2025, the antidepressants segment is expected to hold over two-fifths of the market share. North America's panic attack treatment market is estimated to reach around USD 1 billion in 2025, while Europe is anticipated to hold nearly one-fourth of the global market share. Asia Pacific is expected to show the fastest growth due to increasing anxiety disorders and improved healthcare infrastructure. The prevalence of panic attacks has significantly risen, with a lifetime prevalence of 13.2%. Market growth may be limited by side effects of panic attack treatments, including fatigue, weight gain, and memory issues.
news.headline: Global panic attack treatment market to reach USD 8.66 billion by 2035 with 16.8% CAGR, driven by rising prevalence of panic disorder. Asia Pacific market to record fastest growth.

news.summary: Luminar Technologies, a prominent automotive technology company listed on NASDAQ with stock symbol LAZR, recently disclosed that it has engaged in private agreements with specific holders of its existing 1.25% Convertible Senior Notes due 2026. These agreements involve the acquisition of $50 million in total principal amount of the notes through a combination of repurchases for about $30 million in cash and exchanges for 1.1 million newly issued shares of common stock. The transactions were negotiated separately and are significant for the company's financial operations.
news.headline: Luminar buys back $50M in Senior Notes, reducing debt with $30M in cash and 1.1M shares; cuts capital structure.
